_comment: Norwegian (Bokmål) function-word policy. Same POS-first shape as sl.json. 'pos' lists the closed-class UPOS tags (used when a sentence-context analyzer supplies UPOS — e.g. a future Stanza nb model); under the lowercase lemmatizer (the Phase-1 Norwegian default) UPOS is empty, so 'include' is the SOLE signal and must enumerate the surfaces to cloze. 'include' is focused on the genuine Norwegian difficulties for English speakers: PREPOSITION choice (i/på/til/av/for/med/om/…) and the indefinite ARTICLES en/ei/et (gender + definiteness practice), plus the high-value subordinators som/at. Pronouns and the copula are deliberately left out so /listen doesn't cloze every 'jeg'/'er'. 'clozes_only_verbs' is EMPTY for Norwegian: the mechanism exists for Slovene biti, whose PERSON conjugation (sem/si/je/…) can't be taught from one base card. Modern Norwegian has no person/number agreement — være→er/var/vært is one form per tense — so være/ha are ordinary base-card verbs (er→være, har→ha resolve to the base like kunne/ville). Marking them clozes-only (an earlier copy of the biti pattern) suppressed their real base cards and left be/have resolving to nothing. No T–V distinction and no grammatical case in Norwegian, so unlike sl.json there are no case/dual targets.
